description: Trigger discrimination for the Phase 5 strategy/plan/coverage cluster: automation architecture vs strategy vs per-change plan vs coverage audit, plus the flake-policy vs flake-diagnosis boundary.

cases.prompt: Structure our test codebase, pick the runners, and wire the suites into CI with sensible tiers and retries.
cases.reason: Runners, structure, and CI wiring are automation architecture — the machinery, not the what-to-test decisions.

cases.prompt: Should this product rely on integration tests or E2E for release confidence, and who owns each layer?
cases.reason: Layer investment and ownership derive from the risk inventory — strategy territory; the architect implements the decision afterward.

cases.prompt: This one checkout spec fails every fifth CI run — figure out why and fix it.
cases.reason: Diagnosing a specific flaky test is casework for the detective; the architect only sets the flake policy.

cases.prompt: Before restructuring anything, tell me what our current tests actually cover.
cases.reason: Auditing present coverage is the mapper; architecture consumes its output.
